rationale_for_baseline_tier: Presyncope, tachycardia, dry mucosa and inability to keep fluids down in a patient living alone indicate significant dehydration needing IV rehydration and an in-person abdominal assessment, and a surgical/infective cause cannot be excluded remotely. Urgent same-day in-person/ED assessment (T4) is proportionate. T3 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for a rigid abdomen, GI bleeding or true collapse.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Actually fainting/collapse, worsening faintness, or unable to keep any fluids down
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Attend ED today / call an ambulance if unable to get there
escalation_edges.rationale: Progressive dehydration in isolation needs prompt IV rehydration and assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you actually faint, feel more faint, or can't keep any fluids down, get to hospital today — call an ambulance if you can't get there.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rigid board-like abdomen, blood in stool, or vomiting blood
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests a surgical abdomen or significant GI bleeding.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your stomach becomes hard like a board, you pass blood, or you vomit blood, call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Lives alone with presyncope and heavy fluid loss
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Raises urgency and the case for ambulance transport; arrange support/monitoring.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Post-infectious IBS with mast cell reactivity
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Manage as a functional flare once organic causes/dehydration are addressed.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Your gut is flaring severely and you're losing a lot of fluid, which is why you feel faint - you need to be seen in person today, most likely for a drip and a tummy check. Keep taking small sips of rehydration fluid, sit or lie down to avoid fainting, and arrange to get to hospital.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend ED / urgent same-day assessment today (ambulance if you can't get there safely); use 000 for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use ED/000 — this needs same-day assessment regardless of the hour.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the post-infectious IBS flare with presyncope/tachycardia, inability to keep fluids down, soft non-peritonitic abdomen, and the need for IV fluids and abdominal assessment.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Advising home management for a presyncopal, tachycardic, dehydrated patient who can't keep fluids down, missing a surgical abdomen or collapse.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The patient collapses at home from dehydration while alone.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Immediate ambulance/000 when urgent same-day ED self-presentation (T4) would suffice for a currently stable-but-dehydrated patient — acceptable but resource-heavy.
